title: cerastium octandrum var. humile
intro: Cerastium octandrum var. humile, also known as Creeping Whitlowwort, is a low-growing, spreading plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and burns. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, Creeping Whitlowwort is often used to make infusions, salves, and creams that can be applied topically to the affected area.
